data.title: Seazen Logs 3.2 Billion Yuan in December 2024 Contracted Sales
data.description:
Seazen Group recorded contracted sales of around 3.21 billion yuan in December 2024, with a contracted sales area of 378,700 square meters, a Friday bourse filing said.

In December, the group had 174 properties for lease, with a total gross floor area of a little over 16 million square meters. Rental income for the month stood at approximately 1.09 billion yuan.

For the entire 2024, Seazen's contracted sales and sales area totaled 40.17 billion yuan and 5.4 million square meters, respectively.

data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Reports 58% Surge in December Year-Over-Over Revenue
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co said early Friday that revenue jumped to about 278.16 billion Taiwanese dollars ($8.46 billion) in December from 276.06 billion Taiwanese dollars a month earlier.

The increase represents a gain of 0.8% month-over-month and a surge of almost 58% from a year ago.

Revenue for January through December last year totaled 2.894 trillion Taiwanese dollars, soaring nearly 34% from the same period in 2023. Analysts polled by Factset expected 2.880 trillion Taiwanese dollars.

The analysts' outlook for 2025 is almost 3.657 trillion Taiwanese dollars.

Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor were up 1.4% in premarket activity Friday.

Nvidia and NXP Semiconductors are among the company's top customers, according to data compiled by Factset.

data.title: China Modern Dairy's Unit Issue 200 Million Yuan Short-Term Debentures
data.description:
China Modern Dairy Holdings non-wholly owned subsidiary Modern Farming (Group) issued short-term debentures worth 200 million yuan after receiving approval from the National Association of Financial Market Institutional Investors, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The debentures issued to qualified domestic institutional investors have a 365-day term and a 2.1% interest rate.

data.title: China to Loosen Investment Restrictions for Foreign Companies
data.description:
China's Commerce Ministry plans to loosen restrictions on foreign companies utilizing domestic loans for equity investments, Xinhua News Agency reported Thursday.

The move will help multinational firms set up headquarters-type entities in China, the report said.

data.title: Mercedes-Benz Car Sales Fell 3% in 2024 Amid Challenging Market — Update
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

Mercedes-Benz car sales fell 3% to 1.98 million vehicles in 2024 amid a challenging global market, with top-end vehicle sales weighed by market conditions in China, model changes and weak electric-vehicle demand.

European automakers have faced intense competition with local manufacturers in China in addition to lackluster demand for electric vehicles, while an economic slowdown and property market slump have also seen Chinese buyers shy away from buying high-end cars.

Mercedes has vowed to step up efforts to cut costs and improve efficiency across the business as it seeks to counter softer pricing and lower sales of its higher-margin cars by reducing its cost base.

It had already guided for 2024 car sales to come in slightly below the 2023 level but had said it expected sales in the final quarter of 2024 to come in at a similar level to the third quarter thanks to new combustion engine models and electric versions of its G-Class.

Its sales report released Friday showed car sales rose 3% in the fourth quarter from the third.

"We finished the year with a strong sales quarter, especially with our top-end vehicles and the G-Class in particular," said Britta Seeger, member of the board of management of Mercedes-Benz Group Marketing & Sales.

By region, car sales in its largest market of China fell 7% in 2024 while sales fell 3% in Europe as its top-end vehicles and entry-level models both saw double-digit percentage sales falls.

The German luxury automaker said battery-electric car sales fell 23% in 2024.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: COMMENT-Key market moving events favour these FX options
data.description:
Key FX market-moving events from the United States are on the near-term horizon, making short-dated expiry options an attractive tool to hedge risks and capitalize on potential market over-reactions.

Next Wednesday brings the release of December's U.S. CPI data, followed by December's U.S. retail sales data on Thursday.

Markets will also focus on President-elect Donald Trump's Treasury Secretary nominee, Scott Bessent, who is set to appear before a U.S. Senate committee on Thursday. He might discuss trade tariffs, which have been a strong source previously of FX market volatility.

One-week expiry options would include these events, on top of today's U.S. jobs data, which is already demanding a very high FX volatility risk premium.

If actual FX volatility surpasses the implied volatility priced into a specific option before its expiry, the option could yield a profit, with the risk limited to the upfront premium.

The attached chart shows 1-week and 1-month expiry FX option implied volatility levels compared to their past historic/realised volatility measures.

On this basis, 1-week USD/CAD implied volatility is offering the best potential value, while 1-week EUR/USD might still be worth owning for those who think the impending events will be enough to generate more volatility than was realised over the previous 1-week.

Related

For more click on

data.title: Market Chatter: China's Civil Aviation Sector Returns to Profit in 2024
data.description:
China's civil aviation sector returned to profit in 2024 amid record passenger numbers and strong cargo growth, YiCai Global reported Thursday, citing data from the Civil Aviation Administration of China.

Industry losses decreased by 20.6 billion yuan, with passenger traffic rising 18% to 730 million people and freight and mail volume up 22% to 9 million tons, according to the report.

Compared with pre-COVID levels in 2019, passenger traffic rose 11% and freight and mail volume rose 19%, YiCai Global wrote.

The Civil Aviation Administration of China expected passenger traffic at 780 million people and freight and mail volume at 9.5 million tons in 2025, according to the report.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Heimstaden to Sell Danish Residential Projects in DKK849 Million Deal
data.description:
Heimstaden (HEIM/PREF.ST) sold residential real estate projects in Denmark to Fredensborg 32 for a gross purchase price of 849 million Danish kroner.

Sweden-listed residential property company Heimstaden said Friday the sold projects total 591 units and are newly finalized or ongoing. They are located in the Copenhagen region and Odense.

Heimstaden added that the divestment, which also contains an earn-out consideration, is subject to the successful issue of new senior unsecured notes announced by Heimstaden on Friday.

data.title: Shimao Group Says Dec Contracted Sales Approximately RMB2.83 Bln
data.description:
Shimao Group Holdings Ltd :

  • DEC CONTRACTED SALES APPROXIMATELY RMB2.83 BILLION

data.title: Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Completes Delivery of Submarine to Indian Navy
data.description:
Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders said it has delivered the sixth Scorpene Submarine of Project P-75 on Thursday to the Indian Navy, according to a same-day filing with the Indian bourses.

The submarine will be commissioned into the Indian Navy as INS Vaghsheer, it said.

data.title: Market Chatter: Chinese Property Developers' Bond Sales Fall 18% in 2024
data.description:
Bond sales from China's real estate sector fell 18% in 2024 to 565.31 billion yuan, Yicai Global reported Thursday, citing a study by the China Index Academy.

Bond financing in the sector has been declining since the second half of 2021 but has seen year-on-year growth every month from September 2024 due to a low base effect, the report said.

Economic stimulus by Beijing should help improve market confidence in 2025, the report said, citing the think tank.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: RBC initiates coverage on UK's Liontrust with 'underperform' on recovery concerns
data.description:
** RBC starts coverage of fund manager Liontrust Asset Management with "underperform" rating and 390p PT

** RBC is the only brokerage with equivalent of a "sell" rating and current PT is the lowest among nine analysts covering the stock - LSEG

** Brokerage's PT implies a downside of ~35.9% to its median PT of 672p - LSEG

** Shares of LIO down 1.26% at 430p

** RBC says LIO's assets under management growth will be below that of UK peers - Abrdn , Polar Capital Holdings and Schroders - on lower net flows through CY24

** In October, LIO posted lower second-quarter assets under management and advice

** "Although appearing cheap, given the now relatively subdued flow expectations and earnings recovery, we see limited scope for a near-term re-rating vs peers" - RBC

** However, brokerage says LIO could see better performance compared to its peers if the UK stock market performs well or if returns from sustainable-focused stock investments improve

** LIO shares fell 24.44% in 2024

data.title: UBS downgrades Tele2, cites dividend risks amid bleak outlook
data.description:
** UBS cuts Tele2 to "sell" from "neutral", saying the Swedish telecom operator is unlikely to cover its dividend or reach the cash flow consensus

** The broker expects slowing growth in connectivity service revenues, shutdown of legacy TV and clarity on tax issues in the next few quarters to prompt FCF consensus downgrades

** In Q3, Tele2 reported an increase in tax paid due to SEK 130 million ($12 million) of withheld tax payment

** UBS expects further rating and consensus downgrades before Tele2's longer-term prospects become more promising

** It prefers Nordic peers Telenor and Telia , citing their stronger momentum and scope for consensus upgrades

** Out of 23 analysts covering Tele2, nine rate the stock "strong buy" or "buy", 12 "hold" and two "sell"

($1 = 11.1391 Swedish crowns)

data.title: Subscribe for Laxmi Dental Ltd IPO; Anand Rathi
data.description:
Anand Rathi, Laxmi Dental Ltd

Laxmi Dental Ltd is India’s only end-to-end integrated dental product company as of Sep’24, offering a comprehensive portfolio of dental products. The company operates in 3 verticals i) Laboratory Offerings which include custom made crowns & bridges; ii) Aligner Solutions which includes dental aligners and related branded products and iii) Paediatric Dental Products which include range of products like pre-formed crowns, Silver Diamide Fluoride (SDF), space maintainers, fissure sealants, etc. The company has adopted B2B2C business model for sale of customized clear aligner solutions while offering a flexible ‘pay as you go’ model along with the upfront payment model, making aligners more affordable to the end customers. Adoption of a B2B2C model involves sale of clear aligners through Dental Network who in turn offer dental products to end customers, which has helped it grow rapidly owing to its already established Dental Network with reach of over 22,000 dental clinics, dental companies and dentists between Fiscals 2022 to September 30, 2024.

Valuation and Outlook

The company ranks among the top two dental laboratories in India by revenue and is the leading exporter of dental products. We believe that going forward company’s growth factor could be driven by trends such as the changing regulatory requirements in the medical devices sector helps transition from unorganized to organized players, increasing awareness and demand for dental aesthetics, and the growing preference for metalfree dental products. Hence considering all parameters, we recommend the issue can be consider as “SUBSCRIBE for LONG TERM”.

For all IPO report, click here

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts/broking houses/rating agencies on moneycontrol.com are their own, and not that of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

Laxmi Dental -100125 - anand

data.title: Slovenia Industrial Output Drops in November
data.description:
Industrial production in Slovenia dropped by 0.8% year-on-year in November 2024, slipping from an upwardly revised 3.6% increase in the previous month.

Output declined further for electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply (-17.2% vs -16% in October).

Additionally, production growth slowed both for mining & quarrying (26.1% vs 26.4%) and manufacturing (1% vs 5.9%).

On a monthly basis, industrial activity fell by 1.4%, following an upwardly revised 2.5% gain in the preceding period.

data.title: Fabege sells property in Östermalm to Stadsrum
data.description:
Fabege

Fabege has signed an agreement with Stadsrum Fastigheter for the sale of the Ynglingen 10 property in Östermalm, Stockholm. The sale is being made via companies for an underlying property value of SEK 960m before deductions for deferred tax.

After making deductions for sales costs and deferred tax, the transaction results in a recognised gain of SEK  -36.8m before tax and SEK  91.5m after reversal of deferred tax. This will be recognised on the completion date. Payment will be made in cash on the completion date. The property will be taken over by the buyer in early March.

The property consists of an art nouveau building containing offices, a midwife clinic, shops and a restaurant facing Karlavägen, and a mixed-use property from the 1960s containing rental apartments, offices, a dance school and a car park facing Jungfrugatan and Sibyllegatan.

Ynglingen 10, which is a freehold ownership property, has a lettable area of 11,000 sqm, of which 2,400 sqm is residential. The property is almost fully let and is sold as is. It is certified to BREEAM-SE standard, Very Good.

The sale is part of Fabege's continuing strategy of focusing on selected expansive market areas such as City, Arenastaden, Solna Business Park, Hammarby Sjöstad and Flemingsberg.

Cushman & Wakefield and the Born law firm have been advisors to Fabege.

"The sale is a natural part of our business model. The property is located outside our strategic urban development areas and the transaction also further strengthens our balance sheet and provides financial scope for further investments and acquisitions," says Stefan Dahlbo, CEO of Fabege.

Fabege AB (publ)

For further information, please contact:

Stefan Dahlbo, President and CEO, tel. +46 (0)70-353 18 88, stefan.dahlbo@fabege.se

With a focus on mainly commercial properties, Fabege develops attractive locations in the Stockholm area. We are a present partner that puts people front and centre. Our innovative, responsible and flexible ethos enables companies, locations and our city to develop and thrive. We take a long-term approach in our perspective and ownership. We know that when we create sustainably, we also create value. The Fabege share is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm, in the Large Cap segment. For further information, please visit us at fabege.com.

https://news.cision.com/fabege/r/fabege-sells-property-in-ostermalm-to-stadsrum%2Cc4089560

Fabege sells property in Östermalm to Stadsrum

2015 Ynglingen 10 - Karlavägen 1

data.title: Market Chatter: Hong Kong's Housing Authority Records HK$9 Billion Surplus in Current Fiscal Year
data.description:
Hong Kong's Housing Authority logged HK$9.24 billion in consolidated surplus for the current fiscal year, The Stndard reported Friday.

The consolidated surplus is seen to rise to HK$15.4 billion in the 2025-2026 fiscal year, the report said.

For the upcoming fiscal year, about HK$661 million of the surplus will come from public rental housing, more than HK$1.36 billion of commercial operating accounts and around HK$13.3 billion from the sale of subsidized home-ownership apartments, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: European shares dip as bond yields stay high ahead of US jobs data
data.description:
Key points:
  • France's Ubisoft slides after game delay, guidance cut
  • Deutsche Telekom gains after UBS upgrades to "buy"
  • US Dec. nonfarm payrolls data at 1330 GMT
  • STOXX down 0.1%, though set for third straight weekly gain

European shares edged lower on Friday as elevated government bond yields weighed on stocks, with attention now turning to U.S. jobs data for clues on the Federal Reserve's interest rate trajectory.

The pan-European STOXX 600 was down 0.1% by 0941 GMT, but remained on track for its strongest week in a month.

Yields on European government bonds remained elevated, with the German 10-year bund yield (DE10YT=RR) hitting its highest level in six months. Utilities (.SX6P), often traded as a bond proxy, were down 0.9%.

The U.S. jobs report, due at 1330 GMT, is expected to reveal a moderation in job growth for December. The unemployment rate is likely to stay at 4.2%, which would support the Fed's cautious stance on rate cuts this year.

"Intraday volatility will remain in the next few weeks, both for reason related to rates and politics," said Luca Finà, head of active equity at Generali Asset Management.

"For Europe, tariff news flow is a significant source of volatility but we think that a significant part of this event has been already priced in the market."

European equities and global bond markets faced sell-offs earlier this week amid talks of U.S. President-elect Donald Trump considering a national economic emergency to impose new tariffs.

On Friday, the food and beverages sub-index (.SX3P) was among the top losers, with alcohol manufacturer Pernod Ricard and coffee company JDE Peet's leading losses, while insurance (.SXIP) shed 0.6%.

Helping limit losses, index heavyweight Novo Nordisk gained more than 1%.

Telecoms (.SXKP) gained 0.4%, with Deutsche Telekom up 1.5% after UBS upgraded Europe's biggest telecoms group by market value to "buy" from "neutral".

On the data front, Sweden's economy grew 1.4% in November from the previous month, while Norway's core inflation eased more than expected in December.

Switzerland's unemployment rate rose to a non-seasonally adjusted 2.8% in December from 2.6% in the previous month.

Among individual stocks, French video game maker Ubisoft fell 7.1% after it announced another delay in the newest game in its blockbuster franchise Assassin's Creed franchise and cut its third-quarter and full-year net bookings outlook.

Ambu A/S jumped 13.2% to the top of the pan-European index after the Danish medical devices maker posted preliminary first-quarter results and hiked it full-year outlook.

data.title: PREVIEW: Delta Air expected to report higher rev in results Fri
data.description:
** Delta Air Lines is expected to post a rise in qtrly rev when it releases results before the bell on Fri

** Analysts on an average expect qrtly rev of $14.18 bln vs $13.66 bln a yr ago, and they expect EPS of $1.75 vs $1.28 in the yr-ago qtr, according to LSEG

** Earlier this week, Jefferies raised its price target on Delta, while Bernstein cut its price target on the stock

** The median 12-mo price target on the stock is $77.50, per LSEG

** Delta's stock closed Wed up 0.4% at $61.42. The NYSE and Nasdaq were closed Thurs to remember the life of former President Jimmy Carter

** Of the 23 analysts who cover Delta, 22 rate it a "strong buy" or "buy," one rates it a "hold" and no analysts rate it a "sell" or "strong sell," based on LSEG data

** Delta on Nov. 20 reaffirmed December qtr financial guidance and said it expects 2025 revenue to grow by a mid single-digit percentage as it strengthens its bet on premium travel

** Airline shares rose sharply in 2024 amid robust travel demand

** DAL was up about 50% in 2024, compared with a 23% gain in S&P 500 last year

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
China's Central Bank Suspends Bond Buying as Yields, Yuan Fall

The move comes as long-term yields on Chinese bonds hit fresh lows amid expectations of more monetary policy easing.

French Industry Shows Glimmer of Recovery

Manufacturing output rose 0.2% on month in November, confounding expectations for a third-straight month of decline, according to economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal.

It's Crunch Time for the Bull Market. The Third Year Is the Charm.

If the bull market can survive this critical period, it may thrive for years to come.

Investors are on edge as Friday's jobs report could make or break the stock-market rally

Rising Treasury yields are ratcheting up the anxiety for equity investors. Thursday's stock-market closure offered only a brief reprieve from the mounting tension.

The World's Bond Markets Are Uneasy. It's the U.S.'s Fault.

The yield on 10-year U.K. government debt rose to the highest since 2008, the latest casualty of a global rout spurred by rising yields on Treasury notes.

LA Fires Latest: Death Toll Reaches 10 and Economic Loss Estimates Top $135 Billion.

Firefighters struggled to contain blazes that have destroyed more than 10,000 structures.

U.S. Mortgage Rates Climb to Highest Since July

U.S. mortgage rates are back at their highest level since last summer, frustrating home buyers waiting for a break from some of the steepest borrowing costs in more than a decade.

Why two Fed officials doubt the need for many more rate cuts

One official warned that "progress on inflation could continue to stall."

Bank of England Monitoring Gilt Market, But Sees Moves as Orderly, Says Breeden

"So far the moves have been orderly," Breeden said after a speech in Scotland. "So far so good."

With Port Strike Averted, Dockworkers Draw New Curbs on Automation

A new six-year contract sets a 62% pay increase along with tougher standards for adding robotics to the docks.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
TikTok Is Heading to the Supreme Court. How Meta and Snap Could Benefit from a Ban.

The Supreme Court will hear oral arguments from TikTok on Friday.

The Pacific Palisades Inferno Won't Engulf the Insurance Industry

The insurance industry has been thriving as climate change increases the demand for coverage.

Mercedes-Benz Car Sales Drop Amid Challenging Market

Car sales fell 3% to 1.98 million last year in a challenging global market, with top-end vehicle sales weighed by market conditions in China, model changes and weak electric-vehicle demand.

Fox Must Face Smartmatic's Defamation Lawsuit, Appeals Court Rules

The voting-machine company seeks billions in damages.

BlackRock Withdraws From Climate Coalition, Backpedaling Again on ESG

The world's largest asset manager is latest Wall Street firm to quit climate groups.

Elon Musk's xAI Launches Stand-Alone Grok App

The AI startup launched its first stand-alone consumer app, as the company tries to catch up with more established players such as OpenAI and Google in the generative AI race.

Wells Fargo Taps JPMorgan Executive to Lead Its Credit-Card Business

Growth in credit cards has been a focus for CEO Charlie Scharf.

Delta Air Lines' Earnings Can Make or Break the Sector's Rally. 1 Metric Is Key.

Airline stocks have been flying recently, which makes earnings season tricky to navigate for investors.

Southwest Airlines CFO Tammy Romo to Retire as Airline Embarks on Turnaround

Romo is retiring after more than three decades with the carrier as it works on a turnaround after lagging behind some of its peers in recent years.

DHL Buys Into the Growing Retail Returns Business

The acquisition of a returns services provider comes as retailers are grappling with the higher volumes that shoppers are sending back.

data.title: Inflation Data, Bank Earnings Bonanza: What to Watch Next Week — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

Here's some key corporate earnings, economic data releases, and other events to watch for in the coming week.

Monday, Jan. 13

  • The four-day JPMorgan healthcare conference kicks off. Pfizer's chief executive is among executives participating on the first day; his counterpart at Eli Lilly is due up on Tuesday.

Tuesday, Jan. 14

  • PPI: A December readout from the producer price index, a gauge of wholesale inflation, is due at 8:30 a.m. ET.

Wednesday, Jan. 15

  • CPI report: The consumer-price index for December is due at 8:30 a.m.
  • Bank earnings: Citi, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan and Wells Fargo are all due to report full-year results before the bell.
  • Other notable financial companies reporting include BlackRock and Bank of New York Mellon.

Thursday, Jan. 16

  • Bank earnings, pt. 2: Bank of America and Morgan Stanley are due to report before the open, as are numerous smaller banks including PNC Financial and U.S. Bancorp.
  • Other earnings include UnitedHealth.

Friday, Jan. 17

  • State Street's earnings are due ahead of the market open.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Hisamitsu Pharmaceutical's Nine-Month Profit Balloons 13%
data.description:
Hisamitsu Pharmaceutical recorded a 13% rise in attributable profit for the nine months ended Nov. 30 to 13.7 billion yen from 12.1 billion yen in the year-ago period, a Thursday bourse filing said.

Earnings per share were 184.76 yen in the nine months, up from 156.25 yen in the corresponding period of the last fiscal year.

The pharmaceutical group saw an 8.2% rise in net sales to 111.3 billion yen in the period from 102.8 billion yen a year prior.

The company expects an attributable profit of 20.5 billion yen, basic earnings of 277.66 yen per share, net sales of 154 billion yen, and a total dividend of 90 yen per share for the fiscal year ending Feb. 28, 2025.

data.title: Heimstaden Explores New Notes Issue; Plans Repurchase of Existing Notes
data.description:
Heimstaden (HEIM/PREF.ST) said Friday it mandated JP Morgan, Nordea, Pareto Securities, and Swedbank to explore new fixed-income investors and arrange meetings starting Jan. 13.

The investor meetings will explore issuing new senior unsecured notes in kronor/euro with a 3 to 5-year tenor followed by capital markets transaction, the Swedish residential real estate company said.

Meanwhile, the company launched a tender offer to repurchase 500 million kronor of notes due April 2025, 1.20 billion kronor of notes due October 2025, and 350 million euros of notes due March 2026.

The tender offer, conditional on new note issuance, looks to improve Heimstaden's debt maturity profile and liability composition.

data.title: Middle East Crude-Benchmarks rise, Dubai and Oman at one-week high
data.description:
Middle East crude benchmark premiums all rose on Friday, with that of Oman and Dubai reversing declines from the previous session to hit one-week highs.

Oil prices rose on the day and were on track for a third straight week of gains as traders focused on potential supply disruptions from sanctions, while icy conditions in parts of the United States and Europe are expected to drive up fuel demand.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

Cash Dubai's premium to swaps rose 37 cents to $1.73 a barrel.

Unipec will deliver a March-loading Oman crude cargo to TotalEnergies following the deals.

NEWS

U.S. President Joe Biden's administration expects to release short-term guidance on clean fuel tax credits on Friday and leave final decisions to President-elect Donald Trump's incoming administration, according to two sources familiar with the plan.

The Caspian Pipeline Consortium (CPC), which exports Kazakhstan's oil via the Black Sea, was seen pumping 64.4 million metric tons of oil in 2024 in a latest estimate, further downgrading its exporting prospects, according to a shareholder.

Russian oil producers deployed 12.3% fewer new oil wells in the first 11 months of 2024 compared with a year earlier, owing to rising costs, weaker oil prices and an OPEC+ deal to curb output, Kommersant newspaper reported on Thursday, citing sources familiar with the data.

data.title: Dollar holds onto gains ahead of US jobs data; sterling slips
data.description:
Key points:
  • Dollar eyes 1% weekly gain on soggy sterling
  • Dollar index set for 6th weekly rise
  • US jobs data due as bonds nurse losses
  • Markets expect data to show 160,000 jobs added

The dollar looked set to log a sixth straight week of outperformance against other currencies on Friday, underpinned by elevated bond yields and expectations of another strong set of U.S. job numbers, while sterling continued to slide.

A global sell-off in bonds has dominated markets this week, fuelled by fears of a pick-up in inflation, as well as uncertainty over tariffs under the incoming U.S. administration led by Donald Trump.

This has boosted the dollar, and cast a shadow over other currencies, which have recorded steep losses against the greenback.

U.S. 10-year Treasury yields remained elevated on Friday. The yields have climbed around 10 basis points this week to 4.69%.

Markets were relatively subdued on Friday, jittery ahead of key U.S. non-farm payrolls data, which investors will eye to confirm their view that U.S. rates could stay higher for longer.

"We think the balance of risks is tilted to the upside for the dollar today, as robust job figures could prompt markets to price out a March cut and potentially push the first fully-priced move beyond June," said Francesco Pesole, ING forex strategist.

The pound continued to slide on Friday, and was last down 0.17% at $1.2285.

The dollar has gained 1% this week on the pound, which was battered to a 14-month low on Thursday in tandem with a selloff in gilts and concern about British government finances.

"What makes the current situation particularly noteworthy is that higher interest rates normally help strengthen the currency, so the fact we're seeing the pound weaken even as gilt yields rise goes to demonstrate how nervous investors are right now," Deutsche Bank analysts said in a note.

The dollar has gained 0.3% on the yen this week, but the yen strengthened 0.15% on Friday to 157.82 per dollar.

In the euro zone, the single currency was flat on the day as well as on the week at $1.0299, but was not trading far from the two-year low of $1.0224 it hit last week. ,

A significant number of foreign exchange forecasters expect the euro to reach parity with the dollar in 2025, a Reuters poll showed this week.

This left the dollar index set for a sixth consecutive weekly gain, its longest run since an 11-week streak in 2023.

The index was last flat at 109.14, set for a 0.2% weekly rise.

PAYROLLS

U.S. non-farm payrolls data is expected to show the economy added 160,000 jobs in December on top of the 227,000 in November, with unemployment holding at 4.2%.

Anything stronger would add to the case for fewer Federal Reserve rate cuts and may set off another round of selling in jittery bond markets.

Overnight, Philadelphia Fed President Patrick Harker said he expected the U.S. central bank to cut interest rates, but added that an imminent cut wasn't needed.

Markets expect around 40 bps of Fed rate cuts in 2025.

data.title: Selvaag Bolig ASA: Mandatory notification of trades by primary insider
data.description:
Patrik Eriksson, employee elected director, sold 5 173 shares 10 January. The shares were sold at an average price of NOK 35.59. Eriksson now owns 11 476 shares in Selvaag Bolig ASA.

The shares traded were acquired through the company's share purchase programme for employees and have been sold after the expiry of the lock-up period.

data.title: Apollo considers $9.5 bln investment in Seven & i buyout, Bloomberg says
data.description:
Apollo Global Management is considering investing as much as 1.5 trillion yen ($9.5 billion) in a management buyout of Japan's Seven & i Holdings , Bloomberg News said on Friday, citing several people with knowledge of the matter.

Under the plan, Seven & i's founding family will invest 500 billion yen in equity, trading company Itochu Corp will invest over 1 trillion yen in equity, and Apollo up to 1.5 trillion yen in preferred shares, according to the Bloomberg report.

Apollo did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

In an interview with Reuters on Thursday, Apollo declined to comment on whether it is engaged with parties for a Seven & i deal.

Seven & i said in November it had received a buyout proposal from the founding family, as it weighs a rival offer from Canada's Alimentation Couche-Tard (ATD.D).

If it materialises, the management buyout would be the largest in history.

Since then the founding family has approached a number of private equity firms about supporting their bid, sources have said.

Seven & i, meanwhile, has been looking to hive off non-core businesses, including its supermarket operations, into holding unit York Holdings, which will house 31 subsidiaries including the group's superstores business, baby goods store Akachan Honpo and the company that operates Denny's restaurants in Japan.

Global private equity firms including KKR & Co and Bain Capital submitted non-binding bids for the non-core assets, Reuters reported in December.

On Thursday, the operator of the 7-Eleven convenience store chain said its operating profit fell 24% in the latest quarter, missing analysts' estimates, as inflation hit consumer spending in Japan and North America.

($1 = 158.2300 yen)
